qa_pairs.question: How are software packages installed within the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: Installation of software packages within the Alliance software environment is always performed using scripts.

qa_pairs.question: What tools are utilized for software package installation in the Alliance environment?
qa_pairs.answer: Multiple tools are used, including EasyBuild, Gentoo Overlays (starting in 2020), and NixOS (formerly).

qa_pairs.question: When did the Alliance software environment begin using Gentoo Overlays?
qa_pairs.answer: Gentoo Overlays started being used in 2020.

qa_pairs.question: Is NixOS still actively used for software installation in the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: No, NixOS was formerly used for software installation in the Alliance environment.

qa_pairs.question: Where can the Alliance software environment be accessed and used?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance software environment can be used on any computer in the world.

qa_pairs.question: What technology enables the global use of the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: The software environment can be used on any computer in the world thanks to CVMFS.

qa_pairs.question: How are changes and new installations to the software environment tracked?
qa_pairs.answer: All changes and new installations made to the software environment are tracked through Git.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one view changes to core modules and modules installed for the AVX2 CPU architecture?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes to the core modules and modules installed for the AVX2 CPU architecture can be found at https://github.com/ComputeCanada/easybuild-easyconfigs-installed-avx2/commits/main.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for changes related to modules installed for the AVX512 CPU architecture?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes to the modules installed for the AVX512 CPU architecture are available at https://github.com/ComputeCanada/easybuild-easyconfigs-installed-avx512/commits/main.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check for changes to modules installed for the AVX CPU architecture?
qa_pairs.answer: You can check for changes to the modules installed for the AVX CPU architecture at https://github.com/ComputeCanada/easybuild-easyconfigs-installed-avx/commits/main.

qa_pairs.question: Where are changes to modules installed for the SSE3 CPU architecture documented?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes to the modules installed for the SSE3 CPU architecture are documented at https://github.com/ComputeCanada/easybuild-easyconfigs-installed-sse3/commits/main.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find changes made to the primary configuration files?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes to the primary configuration files are available at https://github.com/ComputeCanada/software-stack-config/commits/main.

qa_pairs.question: What is the link to track changes specific to the EasyBuild configuration?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes to the EasyBuild configuration can be tracked via https://github.com/ComputeCanada/easybuild-computecanada-config/commits/main.

qa_pairs.question: Where are changes to custom modules and scripts recorded?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes to custom modules and scripts are recorded at https://github.com/ComputeCanada/software-stack-custom/commits/main.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for viewing changes to the core of the Gentoo layer?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes to the core of the Gentoo layer, for the module `gentoo/YYYY`, used with `StdEnv/2020` and `StdEnv/2023`, are found at https://github.com/ComputeCanada/gentoo-overlay/commits/main.

qa_pairs.question: Which module and standard environments are associated with changes to the Gentoo layer?
qa_pairs.answer: The changes to the Gentoo layer are for the module `gentoo/YYYY`, used with `StdEnv/2020` and `StdEnv/2023`.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find information regarding changes to the core of the Nix layer, specifically for module `nixpkgs/16.09`?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes to the core of the Nix layer, for the module `nixpkgs/16.09`, used in `StdEnv/2016.4` and `StdEnv/2018.3`, are documented at https://github.com/ComputeCanada/nixpkgs/commits/computecanada-16.09.

qa_pairs.question: Is the tracking of changes for the Nix layer module `nixpkgs/16.09` still current?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the tracking of changes for the core of the Nix layer for module `nixpkgs/16.09` is deprecated.

qa_pairs.question: What standard environments were associated with the deprecated `nixpkgs/16.09` module?
qa_pairs.answer: The deprecated `nixpkgs/16.09` module was used in `StdEnv/2016.4` and `StdEnv/2018.3`.
